entries.title: AtlasDays Pro: Free vs Pro
entries.description: AtlasDays is free to use, with an optional Pro upgrade. See exactly what's free, what AtlasDays Pro unlocks — unlimited trackers, Smart Alerts, exports, US states, and widgets — and how the upgrade works.

entries.title: CSV Import
entries.description: Troubleshoot AtlasDays CSV import: row errors, US State rows, skipped duplicates, overlap warnings, date models, preview review, and when imported data is safe enough to trust.

entries.title: Dashboard and Map
entries.description: Troubleshoot AtlasDays dashboard totals and map output: selected period, Days Abroad, US states, Transit, ongoing trips, and when the record needs fixing.

entries.title: Export and Reports
entries.description: Troubleshoot CSV export and PDF export in AtlasDays: check AtlasDays Pro, selected period, US State columns, missing trips, and what export cannot fix.

entries.title: Flighty Import
entries.description: Import your flight history from a Flighty CSV export into AtlasDays. How trips are built from flights, including US state detection, transit, overnights, and overlaps.

entries.title: Getting Started
entries.description: Avoid early AtlasDays setup mistakes: choose Home Country or No fixed residence correctly, enter trips that affect live counts first, and know when trackers or import should wait.

entries.title: Trip Modes and Record Quality
entries.description: Troubleshoot messy trip history in AtlasDays: choose Exact Dates, Year, Unknown, Transit, and ongoing correctly, and review Photo, CSV, or Flighty import before trusting counts.

entries.title: iCloud Sync and Restore
entries.description: Troubleshoot AtlasDays iCloud sync, new-device and reinstall expectations, what syncs, what does not, and when export is the safer backup path.

entries.title: Photo Import
entries.description: Use AtlasDays Photo Import to scan geotagged photo metadata on device, review inferred trips and US states, handle duplicates and overlaps, and understand privacy.

entries.title: Privacy, Location, and Sync
entries.description: Troubleshoot Auto-Detect Trips, US state detection, Photos access, Flighty or CSV import, notification permission, and iCloud sync expectations.

entries.title: Timeline and Calendar
entries.description: Use the AtlasDays Timeline in List or Calendar mode, find trips and photos, review Auto-Detect suggestions, display US state stays, and resolve overlaps.

entries.title: Trackers and Limits
entries.description: Troubleshoot AtlasDays trackers: check country or US state scope, time window, trip precision, Smart Alerts, and when a tracker result is safe to trust.

entries.title: Home Screen Widgets
entries.description: Add AtlasDays trackers and a world map to your iPhone Home Screen. Choose widget sizes, show tracker forecasts, and understand US state widget limits.

entries.title: 183-Day Tax Residency Rule: What Travelers Need to Know
entries.description: What people usually mean by the 183-day tax residency rule, why it is common but not universal, and where day counting stops being the whole answer.

entries.title: Arizona's 9-Month Residency Presumption Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Arizona's residency rule in plain terms: spending more than nine months (about 270 days) of the tax year in Arizona presumes you are a resident, unless you rebut it. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Australia's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Australia's 183-day test in plain terms: 183 or more days in an income year presumes you are a resident for tax, unless your usual place of abode is outside Australia. How to count it and the official ATO source.

entries.title: California Tax Residency and the Nine-Month Presumption Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: California has no fixed day-count line for residency. It is a closest-connections test, but spending more than nine months (about 270 days) in California in a tax year creates a rebuttable presumption of residency. How the count works, and the official source.

entries.title: Canada's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Canada's 183-day rule in plain terms: sojourn 183 days or more in a calendar year and you are a deemed resident taxed on worldwide income. Plus the residential-ties test, how to count, and the official CRA source.

entries.title: Colombia 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Colombia's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any rolling 365-day period makes you a tax resident. How to count it, what happens when days straddle two years, and the official source.

entries.title: Colorado's Six-Month (183-Day) Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Colorado's six-month rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than six months (more than 183 days) in Colorado in the tax year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Connecticut's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Connecticut's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Connecticut in a taxable year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Country Counting Rules: Every Major List Compared
entries.description: How major country-counting systems differ, what they are actually trying to count, and why travel totals stop being comparable when the list is unclear.
keywords: country counting rules every list compared

entries.title: Digital Nomad Visa Day Limits: A Country-by-Country Overview
entries.description: A practical overview of how common nomad destinations frame short stays, extensions, and longer remote-work routes, without pretending one summary can replace official programme rules.

entries.title: Does a Layover Count as Visiting a Country?
entries.description: How to think about layovers for immigration, day counting, and personal travel lists without pretending those questions all have the same answer.
keywords: does a layover count as visiting a country

entries.title: Estonia 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Estonia's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in any 12 consecutive calendar months makes you a tax resident. How to count it, plus the permanent-home test and the official source.

entries.title: How to Export Your Travel History for a Visa Application
entries.description: What an exported travel-history record is trying to achieve, when it helps, what makes it weak, and what to verify before relying on it for a visa application.

entries.title: Turn Your Flighty Flight History Into a Visa Day Count
entries.description: Flighty tracks your flights, not your days in each country. Here's how to turn your Flighty flight history into a visa and residency day count you can trust.

entries.title: Georgia (Country) 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Georgia's (the country) 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 or more days in any continuous 12-month period ending in the tax year makes you a tax resident. How to count it, plus the official source.

entries.title: Georgia's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule (the US State) Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Georgia the US state taxes you as a resident if you spend 183 days or more in Georgia during any 12 consecutive months, not just a calendar year. How the rolling day count works, and the official source.

entries.title: Greece 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Greece's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any 12-month period makes you a tax resident from your first day. How to count it, the centre of vital interests test, and the official source.

entries.title: Hawaii's 200-Day Tax Residency Presumption Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Hawaii's 200-day rule in plain terms: spending more than 200 days in Hawaii in the tax year creates a rebuttable presumption of residency. How the presumption works, how to rebut it, and the official source.

entries.title: How Many Countries Are There in the World?
entries.description: Why 195 is the usual answer, why travelers still encounter other numbers, and where a simple country count stops being a simple question.
keywords: how many countries in the world

entries.title: How to Track Your Travel Days (And Why Spreadsheets Fail)
entries.description: What tracking travel days is actually trying to achieve, why spreadsheets and memory fail faster than people expect, and what makes a travel record usable over time.

entries.title: Idaho's 270-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Idaho's 270-day rule in plain terms: keep a home in Idaho and spend more than 270 days there in the tax year and you are a resident, even if domiciled elsewhere. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Ireland's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Ireland's tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in a tax year makes you resident, and a 280-day two-year test can too. How to count it and the official source.

entries.title: Japan's 90-Day Rule Explained
entries.description: What people usually mean by Japan's 90-day rule, how Japan differs from Schengen-style rolling windows, and why repeated entries are not the same as a clean annual allowance.

entries.title: Maine's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Maine's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Maine in a tax year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, the safe harbors, and the official source.

entries.title: Malta 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Malta's tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in a calendar year makes you a Maltese tax resident. How to count it, what domicile and the remittance basis change, and the official source.

entries.title: Maryland's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Maryland's 183-day rule in plain terms: a place of abode kept for more than six months plus 183 or more days in Maryland in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Massachusetts's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Massachusetts's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Massachusetts in the taxable year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Mauritius 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Mauritius's tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in an income year makes you a Mauritian tax resident, and a separate 270-day test looks across three years. How to count both, and the official source.

entries.title: Minnesota's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Minnesota's 183-day rule in plain terms: an abode in Minnesota plus at least 183 days in the state during the year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Nebraska's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Nebraska's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus 183 days or more in Nebraska in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: New Jersey's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: New Jersey's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent home in New Jersey plus more than 183 days in the state in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: New York's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: New York's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in New York in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: New Zealand 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: New Zealand's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any 12-month period makes you a tax resident, backdated to the first of those days. How to count it, plus the permanent-place-of-abode test and the official source.

entries.title: North Dakota's 7-Month (210-Day) Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: North Dakota's 7-month rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 210 days in North Dakota in a tax year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Ohio's 212 Contact-Period Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Ohio counts contact periods (overnights), not days. More than 212 contact periods in a calendar year makes you a presumed Ohio resident under Rev. Code §5747.24. How contact periods work, and the official source.

entries.title: Oregon's 200-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Oregon's 200-day rule in plain terms: maintaining a place of abode in Oregon plus more than 200 days in the state during the tax year makes you a resident, unless the stay is temporary. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Overstaying a Visa or Stay Limit: What It Means and Why It Matters
entries.description: What overstaying a visa or stay limit means, why consequences differ across immigration systems, and the key misunderstandings that put travelers at risk.
keywords: overstaying a visa or stay limit

entries.title: Pennsylvania's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Pennsylvania's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Pennsylvania in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Portugal 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Portugal's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any rolling 12-month period makes you a tax resident. How to count it, plus the habitual-residence test and the official source.

entries.title: How to Prove Time Spent in a Country When You Don't Have Perfect Records
entries.description: What proving time spent in a country is actually trying to do, how stronger and weaker evidence work together, and how to build a defensible chronology when records are incomplete.
keywords: prove time spent in country without perfect records

entries.title: How to Prove You Were Not in a Country - AtlasDays
entries.description: What proving absence is actually trying to establish, which records can support it, and why a consistent chronology matters more than any one document.
keywords: prove you were not in a country

entries.title: How to Rebuild Your Travel History From Passport Stamps, Emails, and Photos
entries.description: What rebuilding travel history actually involves, which sources are strongest, how to handle gaps and conflicts honestly, and what makes a reconstructed timeline defensible.
keywords: rebuild travel history from passport stamps emails photos

entries.title: Rhode Island's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Rhode Island's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Rhode Island in a calendar year makes you a resident, even if domiciled elsewhere. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Romania 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Romania's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any 12 consecutive months ending in the calendar year makes you a tax resident. How to count it, plus the domicile and centre of vital interests tests and the official source.

entries.title: What Does 90 Days in Any 180-Day Period Mean?
entries.description: The Schengen 90-day rule in Europe means up to 90 total days in the Schengen area within any rolling 180-day period. Plain-English explanation and examples.

entries.title: Schengen Countries List for the 90/180 Rule
entries.description: Which countries count together under the Schengen 90/180 short-stay rule, and why EU membership is not the same thing as Schengen membership.

entries.title: How the Schengen Rolling Window Actually Works
entries.description: A practical walkthrough of how the Schengen 90/180 rolling window works, why days return one by one, and where manual counting goes wrong.

entries.title: Schengen Single-Entry Visa: Entries vs Duration of Stay – AtlasDays
entries.description: What a Schengen single-entry visa means: how to read the number of entries, the duration of stay, and the validity dates on a uniform (type C) visa. Once you leave, a single-entry visa is used up.

entries.title: Singapore 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Singapore's tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more of presence or employment in a calendar year makes you a Singapore tax resident. How to count it, the concessions, and the official source.

entries.title: Thailand 180-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Thailand's tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 180 days in a calendar year makes you a Thai tax resident. How to count it, what it means for foreign income, and the official source.

entries.title: How to Prepare Travel History for a Visa Application
entries.description: What a travel-history record for a visa application usually needs to do, what makes it usable or weak, and where official application instructions take priority.

entries.title: Travel History Template for Visa Applications
entries.description: What a travel-history template is for, why no single template works everywhere, what fields to include, and what to verify before relying on one in a formal submission.

entries.title: Turkiye's 90/180 Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Turkiye's 90/180 short-stay rule in plain terms: at most 90 days within any rolling 180-day period on a visa or visa exemption. How the rolling window is counted, with a worked example and the official source.

entries.title: UAE 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: The UAE's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in any consecutive 12-month period makes you a UAE tax resident. How to count it, the 90-day test, and the official source.

entries.title: UK Citizenship Absence Limits: What Naturalisation Residence Rules Are Trying to Show - AtlasDays
entries.description: What UK citizenship absence limits are trying to assess, how the 5-year and 3-year naturalisation residence tests are usually framed, and why timing, status history, and discretion matter.

entries.title: UK ETA vs Standard Visitor Visa: What's the Difference?
entries.description: How the UK ETA and Standard Visitor visa differ, when the distinction matters, and why neither one should be treated as guaranteed entry by itself.
keywords: uk eta vs standard visitor visa

entries.title: UK ILR Absence Limits: What Continuous Residence Is Trying to Show - AtlasDays
entries.description: What UK ILR absence limits are trying to measure, why many routes use a 180-days-in-any-12-months style test, and why route, qualifying period, and rule version matter.

entries.title: UK Standard Visitor: How Length of Stay Is Assessed
entries.description: What people usually mean by the UK Standard Visitor 180-day shorthand, why it is incomplete, and how visit length, travel pattern, and genuine visitor assessment fit together.

entries.title: UK Statutory Residence Test: What Travelers Need to Know - AtlasDays
entries.description: What the UK Statutory Residence Test is trying to determine, why it is not a simple 183-day rule, and how automatic tests, sufficient ties, and prior residence change the analysis.

entries.title: US B1/B2 Visa: Understanding the 180-Day Stay Limit
entries.description: What people usually mean by the U.S. B1/B2 180-day stay shorthand, why it is incomplete, and how visa validity, admission, and authorized stay fit together.
keywords: us b1 b2 visa 180 day limit

entries.title: US Substantial Presence Test: What Travelers Need to Know
entries.description: What the U.S. substantial presence test is trying to determine, why it is not a simple 183-day rule, and where exceptions, treaties, and professional advice matter.

entries.title: US Visa Waiver Program 90-Day Limit Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: The US Visa Waiver Program in plain terms: with an approved ESTA you may be admitted for up to 90 days per visit. How the limit works, why nearby-country trips don't reset it, and the official source.

entries.title: Vermont's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Vermont's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Vermont in the taxable year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: Virginia's 183-Day Actual Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
entries.description: Virginia's 183-day rule in plain terms: maintaining a place of abode in Virginia for more than 183 days in the taxable year makes you an actual resident, even if domiciled elsewhere. How to count it, and the official source.

entries.title: What Counts as a 'Day' for Visa Purposes?
entries.description: What people usually mean when they ask what counts as a day, why the answer is not universal, and where entry, exit, transit, and regime differences start to matter.
keywords: what counts as a day for visa purposes

entries.title: When Do You Get Schengen Days Back After a 90-Day Stay?
entries.description: When Schengen days come back after a long stay, why they return one by one, and why there is no lump-sum reset.
keywords: when do you get schengen days back after 90 day stay
